expectations.text: behavioral_check: Names `speed_smoothing_std_dev` (Gaussian sigma) as the field that controls speed-trace noise
expectations.evidence: Table row: 'speed_smoothing_std_dev | 0.100 s | Gaussian sigma inside get_speed (LED-speed outlier rejection) and get_velocity (the velocity used for speed)'. Identifies it as the one driving the speed column.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Distinguishes `speed_smoothing_std_dev` (Gaussian sigma applied at the velocity stage inside `get_velocity`, common_position.py:475) from `position_smoothing_duration` (moving-average applied to the LED positions BEFORE centroid / velocity / speed are computed, common_position.py:431-435). Both affect the returned `speed` column — they just enter the pipeline at different stages — and shrinking either produces a noisier speed trace. Does NOT claim either field is decoupled from `speed`, and does NOT claim `position_smoothing_duration` is applied after speed is computed.
expectations.evidence: Position_smoothing_duration described as 'Moving-average window applied to front_LED and back_LED positions before centroid/velocity (common_position.py:432-438)'; speed_smoothing_std_dev as Gaussian sigma in get_velocity. Both clearly affect speed.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Predicts: shrinking / disabling `speed_smoothing_std_dev` gives a noisier speed trace, causing spurious crossings of downstream thresholds
expectations.evidence: States 'speed will track the raw LED differences much more closely, including any 1-frame jumps'; concluding paragraph: 'downstream code that thresholds on speed will misbehave if you feed it the raw version'.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Recommends inspecting `TrodesPosParams.describe()` / `.heading` for exact field names rather than guessing
expectations.evidence: Does not recommend `.describe()` or `.heading`; instead cites source code lines and `default_params`. The field-name discovery surface explicitly asked for is missing.
